    Home › Lines › Maersk

    MSK Maersk

    Denmark · Gemini Cooperation · HQ: Copenhagen

    The Danish giant that defined modern container shipping — and now runs the Gemini network with Hapag-Lloyd.

    Ships here3
    Biggest shipMærsk Mc-Kinney Møller
    Group / allianceGemini Cooperation
    Founded1904
    MarkMSK
    Side by side

    The fleet at true scale

    Every giant Maersk operates on this site, drawn to the same scale — longest hull on the left. Tap any silhouette.

    Mærsk Mc-Kinney Møller
    Madrid Mærsk
    Emma Mærsk
    The fleet

    What Maersk operates

    Grid
    1 in fleet

    Mærsk Mc-Kinney Møller

    Hanwha Ocean (DSME) · In service

    Asia–Europe network mainstay since 2013.

    Full specification →
    1 in fleet

    Madrid Mærsk

    Hanwha Ocean (DSME) · In service

    Asia–Europe service; now operating within the Gemini network with Hapag-Lloyd.

    Full specification →
    1 in fleet

    Emma Mærsk

    Odense Steel Shipyard · In service

    Two decades on the Asia–Europe trade; still in Maersk service.

    Full specification →
    Good to know

    Maersk, in a few facts

    • Held the world's-largest-line title for a quarter of a century before MSC overtook it in 2022.
    • Launched the Gemini Cooperation with Hapag-Lloyd in 2025 — a hub-and-spoke network built around schedule reliability above 90%.
    • Its Triple-E class made fuel efficiency, not speed, the design goal of every giant that followed.
    Sources

    Primary

    • Maersk — about ↗

    Further reading

    • Maersk — Wikipedia ↗

Port safety Ships in storm The Silhouette Quiz Methodology Matchups Icon vs Titanic Legend vs Icon Titanic vs Queen Mary 2 Seawise Giant vs Icon Ever Given vs Ever Alot Emma Mærsk vs MSC Irina Blog The six days that stopped world trade Why the biggest ships stopped growing
    About the data. Every specification is compiled from operator, builder and registry sources and cited per row. Fleet and status figures are snapshots, marked ≈ where approximate and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. Ranks never mix units — cruise ships rank by GT, container ships by TEU, tankers by DWT. All silhouettes are original works of ships.fyi, drawn to true scale. Guides may contain clearly marked affiliate links; they never change what we recommend. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
    © 2026 ships.fyi
    RecordsExplained
